title: Type Inference
content:
# TypeScript Interview Prep

## Q1: What is the difference between standard Interface merging and Type aliases?

### Standard Answer
Interfaces support **Declaration Merging**: if you declare two interfaces with the same name in the same scope, the TS compiler automatically merges their members. This is useful for extending library modules. Type aliases do not support declaration merging; attempting to declare two types with identical identifiers in the same scope throws compile-time errors.


## Q2: How does the system handle concurrent throughput anomalies under Type Inference?

### Standard Answer
The system uses dynamic resource pools and queue-backoff throttles to maintain throughput boundaries, avoiding thread starvation.
